lesson.menu_desc: Social dinners and invitations: inviting, accepting and declining -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Building Relationships -- Social Dinners & Invitations (Would you like...? / I'd love to / I'm afraid I can't)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 15 -- Building Relationships

listenings.text: Hi Marcos, this is Daniel from the valuation panel. I really enjoyed our talk this morning, and a few of us are getting together for dinner tonight. We have a table booked at an Italian place near the venue, around seven thirty. It would be great if you could join us. The food is excellent, and it is a relaxed way to keep the conversation going after the sessions. Are you free this evening? If you can make it, just send me a quick message and I will share the address. I hope you can come. Talk soon.

Hi Marcos, thank you so much for the invitation to dinner on Friday. It is very kind of you to think of me. I would love to come, and I am looking forward to it. Seven thirty works perfectly for me, and the restaurant you suggested sounds wonderful. Should I bring anything, or shall we just meet there? Also, my colleague Sarah asked if she could join us. She is free that evening too. Please let me know if there is room at the table. See you on Friday, and thanks again for the kind invitation.
